decisions.decision: opencti healthcheck: node -e HTTP check (exit 0 if statusCode < 500)
decisions.rationale: curl not in opencti image; wget exits non-zero on 401; node is available and handles auth responses correctly
No curl/wget in ollama image; ollama binary is the only reliable readiness probe available
No curl/wget/python3 in chromadb image; bash builtin TCP check is only available tool

decisions.decision: init-models.sh readiness check: docker compose exec ollama ollama list
decisions.rationale: Port 11434 is internal-only (not host-bound by design); host-side curl can never reach it

decisions.decision: verify-platform.sh health poll: curl -s + grep (dropped -f flag)
decisions.rationale: OpenCTI /health returns HTTP 401 when healthy; -f flag treats 4xx as failure causing infinite loop
OpenCTI 6.x requires a separate worker process to consume RabbitMQ and index into Elasticsearch; without it STIX bundles queue forever and ATT&CK count stays 0
Missing env var caused get_config_variable() to return None; int(None) crash on line 105 of connector.py caused 10s crash-loop

next_action: Run './scripts/verify-platform.sh' — worker is processing queue. When it prints '[verify-platform] Phase 1 complete.' type 'phase-1-complete' to close the plan.
context_notes: Platform is fully operational with 9 services (added worker). All healthcheck bugs fixed — root cause was curl -f/-sf used against services that return HTTP 4xx when healthy. The complete set of fixes in commit 4b79772: opencti/ollama/chromadb healthchecks rewritten to use native tools, init-models.sh readiness check moved inside container, verify-platform.sh health poll drops -f flag, worker service added, MITRE_INTERVAL added. The MITRE ATT&CK import is in flight — connector sent data at 04:52Z, worker started at 04:57Z. After phase-1-complete: write SUMMARY, run gsd-verifier, then plan Phase 2 (Feed Ingestion) and Phase 3 (AI IOC Extraction) in parallel.
